Title: (+)-Sesamin-oxidising CYP92B14 shapes specialised lignan metabolism in sesame

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constant throughout S. radiatum seed development
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; co-expression in yeast bioassays with CYP92B14 proteins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CYP92B14 catalytic assays as electron donor, but no direct nutrient-metabolite causal effect tested as focal gene
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrCPR1 was identified from S. radiatum seed RNA-seq. 2. Its expression remained relatively constant during seed development. 3. It was used to support heterologous CYP92B14 activity assays rather than being a primary causal gene for lignan profile.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally used in the Results context, but it is not the main metabolic determinant in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value alone; useful as accessory redox partner in heterologous reconstruction of lignan P450 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: (+)-sesamin oxidation branch leading to sesaminol- and sesamolin-derived lignans
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (+)-sesamin to mainly (+)-sesaminol via both direct aromatic-ring oxidation and oxidative rearrangement of α-oxy-substituted aryl groups (ORA), and produces low levels of (+)-sesamolin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and determines branch allocation between co-products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Pivotal branch-point enzyme; key control step for shaping product ratio
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of (+)-sesaminol-derived lignans and strongly biases metabolism away from (+)-sesamolin in S. radiatum
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized seed lignans with antioxidant/nutritional relevance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated at 35 DAP, peaks at 42 DAP, then rapidly decreases at 49 DAP in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing S. radiatum seeds, especially late seed development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stage; heterologous yeast assay with (+)-sesamin substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification and expression profiling; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous yeast expression with SiCPR1; HPLC product profiling; isotope-labeled substrate tracing by NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrCYP92B14 is a SiCYP92B14 orthologue identified from developing S. radiatum seeds. 2. It oxidizes (+)-sesamin predominantly to (+)-sesaminol, with only low/trace formation of (+)-sesamolin. 3. Isotope-labeling showed it uses both direct oxidation (71%) and ORA (29%) to form (+)-sesaminol. 4. Its catalytic preference explains the predominance of sesaminol-derived lignans such as (+)-sesangolin in S. radiatum seeds. 5. Therefore it is a key enzyme shaping lignan product ratios in wild sesame.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of sesame or heterologous platforms to increase sesaminol-derived lignans such as sesangolin; can be deployed to shift branch flux away from sesamolin.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce sesamolin accumulation, which could lower sesamol-derived antioxidant potential in oil products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also linked to biosynthesis of the novel lignan (+)-7'-episesantalin indirectly through sesaminol/ORA-derived chemistry; product ratio is influenced by active-site residues.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: (+)-sesamin oxidation branch leading to sesamolin and sesaminol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (+)-sesamin to (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol through multiple reaction schemes including oxidative rearrangement of α-oxy-substituted aryl groups and direct oxidation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and determines branch allocation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Pivotal branch-point enzyme; key control step for product-ratio determination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes (+)-sesamolin accumulation over (+)-sesaminol and helps shape cultivated sesame lignan profile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized seed lignans with antioxidant and nutritional relevance; sesamolin can yield sesamol contributing to oil stability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previous and current yeast expression assays; product profiling; deuterium-labeled substrate NMR tracing; mutational analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiCYP92B14 is the cultivated-sesame orthologue serving as the benchmark enzyme in this study. 2. It allocates (+)-sesamin to (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol, favoring (+)-sesamolin. 3. Isotope tracing showed product formation occurs mainly via ORA, especially toward (+)-sesamolin (82%). 4. Amino-acid substitutions can shift its product ratio toward sesaminol, demonstrating active-site control of lignan branch outcome.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for breeding/editing to tailor sesamolin vs sesaminol composition in sesame seed oil; useful for improving desired antioxidant or nutraceutical lignan profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifting away from sesamolin may reduce downstream sesamol-related oil stability benefits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant forms identified residues affecting co-product ratio: T121, A313, S317, T318 and Block190-related region.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sesamin biosynthesis from (+)-pinoresinol via piperitol/sesamin synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for CYP92B14-dependent oxidation products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the (+)-sesamin substrate pool for downstream sesaminol- and sesamolin-derived lignans
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in latter half of seed development and peaks at 42 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing S. radiatum seeds during late seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling in this paper; prior functional identification cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrCYP81Q2 expression parallels SrCYP92B14 during seed development. 2. The coordinated expression suggests joint participation in lignan biosynthesis. 3. It likely provides (+)-sesamin substrate for SrCYP92B14 in S. radiatum seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with CYP92B14 to increase total flux into desired lignan branches.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function was not newly characterized here; included because it is a directly discussed upstream pathway enzyme in Results.
